verified.name: Wolne Lektury
verified.tagline: Polish free-library books, authors and epochs as JSON
verified.description: A keyless catalog API for WolneLektury.pl, Poland's free digital library of public-domain and open-licensed literature. Query books, authors, genres and literary epochs, each returning tidy JSON with cover art, colors and download links. The full book list is a multi-megabyte payload, so lean on the smaller collection endpoints for quick calls.
verified.dataLicense: Works are public domain or on free/Creative Commons licenses (per WolneLektury); API terms not formally stated
verified.notes: The unfiltered /books/?format=json response is ~5MB and can exceed short timeouts; smaller endpoints like /epochs/, /genres/ or a single book slug respond quickly.

verified.name: Rig Veda
verified.tagline: Structured metadata for the hymns of the Rig Veda
verified.description: Turns the Rig Veda's corpus of Vedic hymns into queryable JSON: which gods are praised, which poets composed the verses, and which metrical patterns they follow. Endpoints slice the material by mandala, deity, poet, meter or category, with an expanded mode for hymn-level detail. It runs on a free Render instance, so the first request after an idle period may cold-start before later calls settle around one second.
verified.notes: Part of the 'indica' project; hosted on Render's free tier — expect a ~30-50s cold start on the first hit after idle, then ~1s responses. CORS echoes the request Origin.

verified.name: Bank of Russia
verified.tagline: Central Bank of Russia daily FX rates, served as JSON
verified.description: A community JSON mirror of the Bank of Russia's official daily exchange-rate fixing, which the central bank itself publishes only as XML/SOAP. A single GET returns every listed currency's ruble value alongside the previous day's rate, making day-over-day deltas trivial. No key, CORS-open, and refreshed each business day around 11:30 Moscow time.
verified.dataLicense: Unverified (underlying rates are official Bank of Russia public data)
verified.notes: This is the cbr-xml-daily.ru JSON mirror, not an official cbr.ru endpoint; the official Central Bank of Russia service only exposes XML. Despite the .js extension the body is JSON.

verified.name: Excuser
verified.tagline: Random ready-made excuses, tagged by category, as JSON
verified.description: A tiny novelty API that hands back a random excuse whenever you need one, sorted into categories like funny, office and family. Each call returns a small JSON array with an id, the excuse text and its category. The original Heroku deployment retired, so it now lives on a Vercel host.

sampleJson.excuse: My car radio was broken, and I couldn't drive if I didn't have music.

verified.notes: The listed excuser.herokuapp.com docs host is dead (Heroku free dynos retired); the working API is now served from excuser-three.vercel.app.
